# UI / UX Phase 1 Prompt: Entry Surfaces

```text
Use English.

This file captures the landing-page direction that has already been started so future work does not drift away from it.

Status:
- This phase has already been partially implemented.
- Treat this as a reference prompt, not the next phase to execute by default.

Goals of this phase:
- Make the entry experience feel calm, modern, and deliberate.
- Position the product as guided interview practice rather than a noisy challenge site.
- Introduce timed challenge formats as premium-looking product surfaces.

Files already tied to this phase:
- frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.js
- frontend/src/App.css

Decisions already made:
- The landing-side card now highlights timed challenge previews.
- The timed challenge formats are:
  - 30-minute challenge: 1 easy + 2 medium
  - 60-minute challenge: 1 easy + 2 medium + 1 hard
- Those surfaces should remain preview tiles for now unless a later phase intentionally activates them.
- The copy should keep samples sandboxed and the main challenge path persistent.

Visual direction to preserve:
- quieter editorial hero copy
- stronger hierarchy between the main challenge CTA and the sample path
- timed formats presented as intentional product offers, not as marketing cards
- minimal, premium rhythm instead of badge-heavy landing clutter

If this phase is revisited later:
- keep the timed challenge tiles
- keep the challenge mixes above
- make only small coherence refinements that help match the later app-shell design
- do not turn the landing page into a dense dashboard
```
